summary: Projection of canonical private planning event: app-local workflow adopted with one enabled base shell, six explicitly disabled nearby feature families, and remote peer media absent and inert.
Rusty Quest feature, main, and dev were atomically advanced to the accepted workflow-adoption commit; compact dirty and pending-push state was cleared.
MOD-001 activated for particle-family classification and scoped source-impact mapping; implementation moves, runtime activation, and device work remain out of scope.

Particle classification selected existing Matter contracts, preserved Lattice/Optics/Quest/app boundaries, named native-renderer as independent consumer, and entered validation without moving implementation.
MOD-001 accepted after Matter, Lattice, Optics, Quest app, workflow, and public-boundary gates passed; MOD-002 is the sole next-ready unit.
MOD-002 activated for owner-repo conformance fixtures over existing Matter contracts; platform code, feature activation, and live devices remain out of scope.
MOD-002 accepted after Matter, Lattice, and Optics conformance and leakage gates passed; MOD-003 is the sole next-ready unit while the coordinated particle contract batch is pending push.

The MOD-001/MOD-002 particle contract batch was pushed source-first across Matter, Lattice, Optics, portable workflow, and Quest; compact dirty and pending-push state is clear and MOD-003 remains sole next-ready.
MOD-003 activated for the explicit Quest particle adapter, Spatial consumer, native-renderer independent consumer, rollback profile, and later serial-scoped two-Quest evidence.

Opened the additive corrective unit for one Rust/JNI lock authority, Native Renderer effect-closure admission, stale-decision revocation, and append-only evidence preservation without rewriting MOD-003 history.
MOD-006 entered source validation; device acceptance and stable promotion remain explicit pending gates.

Rust/JNI/Kotlin authority parity, rejected-start closure, stale-lock revocation, Native hand/particle effect closure, APK builds, workflow self-tests, tracked-history restoration, and repository-wide gates passed; two-Quest runtime acceptance remains pending.

Additively superseded the still-active historical MOD-003 projection after the completion re-audit opened MOD-006; the first nine events, candidate files, and absent local MOD-004/MOD-005 history remain unchanged, while MOD-006 is the sole in-flight corrective unit.

Proposed explicit broker-media-client adoption with a declared shell dependency, app feature-lock identity, Manifold session authority, Spatial render-adoption authority, and per-client lifecycle/lease closure; MOD-006 remains the sole active device-pending unit.
Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.
Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.
